title: conostylis stylidioides
intro: Conostylis stylidioides, also known as the sticky triggerplant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Haemodoraceae. The sticky triggerplant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, skin irritations, and minor infections due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing skin conditions, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Conostylis stylidioides can be made into a salve or ointment to be applied topically, or infused in hot water to create a tea for internal use.
